matches.x: And Abraham added and took a wife, and her name was Keturah; she is Hagar, who had been bound to him from the beginning.

matches.lb: Quoting Commentary: Tze'enah Ure'enah, Chayei Sarah 90
matches.x: Bahya writes. Keturah was Hagar. That is why the verse says, “Abraham again took a wife” [25:1]. Abraham again took a wife. Why does it have to say again, but could say, he took a wife. The explanation is that it says again because he had her as a wife previously and he was taking her again.83Bahya, Genesis, 25:6.

Rabbi Yehuda said, Even if we came only for this secret, it would have sufficed. They said to him, Happy is your lot in the World to Come, for no secret is withheld from you. They said to him, We came before you, Master, to know the secret of the verse, "Then again Abraham took a wife, and her name was Keturah" (Gen. 25:1).

Rabbi Yehuda said, Now this whole portion of the scripture is made clear, but I do not know the meaning of the verse, "Then again Abraham took a wife, and her name was Keturah" (Gen. 25:1). To a reasonable mind, this text is contradictory. It contradicts the explanation concerning the soul and body at the time of resurrection.
Avraham again took a wife. Her name was Keturah.
And Abraham added and took a wife, and her name was Keturah; she is Hagar, who had been bound to him from the beginning.

These are the years of the life of Ishmael: He lived for one hundred and thirty-seven years; and he expired and died; and he was gathered to his people.

matches.lb: Quoting Commentary: Covenant and Conversation; Genesis; The Book of the Beginnings, Toldot, On Clones and Identity 7
matches.x: And these are the generations of Isaac, son of Abraham: Abraham begat Isaac. (25:19)

AND THESE ARE THE GENERATIONS OF ISAAC, ABRAHAM’S SON. ‘Why’, asked R. Jose, ‘should Isaac be referred to just here, and here only, as “Abraham’s son”? The reason is that with the death of Abraham Isaac assumed the image of his father, so that anyone looking at him could say “This is surely Abraham”, and thus would be convinced that “Abraham begat Isaac”.’
And these are the generations of Isaac son of Abraham. We relate Isaac to Abraham to fulfill what is stated, "And the glory of children is their parents." (Proverbs 17:6)

The term vayithrozzu (and they struggled) is akin to a root meaning “to break”,…

I’m famished!”… Jacob replied, “First sell me your birthright.” “Look, I am about to die,” Esau said. “What good is the birthright to me?” But Jacob said, “Swear to me first.” So he swore an oath to him, selling his birthright to Jacob. Then Jacob gave Esau some bread and some lentil stew. He ate and drank, and then got up and left. So…
